title: MEDIUM: Stored HTML rendered without re-sanitization on output
description: In src/activitypub.rs, bio_html (line 375) and content_html (lines 361, 483) are stored pre-sanitized and then interpolated directly into HTML templates without re-sanitization at render time. This relies on write-time sanitization being correct and the DB not being modified externally. If a code path ever stores unsanitized HTML (e.g., a bug in import, a migration, or direct DB edit), this becomes stored XSS. Defense-in-depth: re-sanitize at render time, or at minimum re-escape HTML-special characters. This is a 'trust the DB' assumption that is fragile. Files: /home/mark/PROJECT/smallhold/src/activitypub.rs lines 361, 375, 483.
close_reason: Acceptable risk: write-time sanitization is correct. DB is trusted per threat model. Re-sanitization on every read would add latency for no real benefit.
